text:
First, note: US/Eastern and Europe/Paris time zones in 1594 did not have modern DST or standardized time zones.  
But historically, Paris mean time was UTC+0:09:21 (approx. UTC+0:09) before 1911, and US Eastern standard time is roughly UTC-5 today, but in 1594 time zones didn’t exist; local mean time was used.  
Assuming the question intends modern time zone offsets for simplicity:  
US/Eastern = UTC-5 (EST, no DST in August? Actually DST was not used in 1594, so EST year-round).  
Europe/Paris = UTC+1 (CET, no DST in August historically for 1594).  

Difference: UTC+1 − (UTC-5) = 6 hours ahead.  
2:00 AM August 21, 1594 EST + 6 hours = 8:00 AM August 21, 1594 in Paris.  

FINAL_ANSWER: 08:00
